description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent metal hoops—one at the top, one at the middle, and one at the bottom. The wood appears aged, with visible grain, knots, and subtle weathering, giving it a rustic and authentic look. The metal hoops are dark and riveted, providing structural reinforcement and a touch of industrial character. The top surface is flat and closed, with no visible opening or bung, suggesting it is sealed. There are no handles or spouts, and the surface is free of any labels, engravings, or decorative markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops and a closed, flat top, showing rustic wood grain and no visible markings or handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, the wood grain, and all three metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with similar features, but the wood grain and knots are more pronounced on the left side.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, highlighting a subtle notch or imperfection on the top rim and a slightly different wood pattern.
description_view_3: Back view with the metal hoops and wood grain visible, showing a smoother wood surface with fewer knots.
